$comment: c114 (D-WIN64-PDATA-XDATA-UNWIND): the runtime witness that DSS's pe64 .pdata (RUNTIME_FUNCTION) + .xdata (UNWIND_INFO) tables are CORRECT — verified by the OS itself. main takes the address of target_fn and calls kernel32 RtlLookupFunctionEntry, which binary-searches .pdata (via the EXCEPTION data directory it points at) for the entry covering that address; the program asserts the found entry's BeginAddress RVA equals target_fn's own RVA (pc - imageBase), returning 42 iff the OS located the right entry. A missing/malformed .pdata makes RtlLookupFunctionEntry return NULL -> 91; a wrong table -> 93. pe64-ONLY: RtlLookupFunctionEntry + the x64 unwind format are Windows (windows.json is availableObjectFormats:[pe]); no elf/macho arm. RED-on-disable: remove the pe.cpp .pdata/.xdata emission -> NULL -> exit 91 (not 42). The `release` arm proves the optimizer preserves the emitted tables end-to-end.
